Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails for NorthWind-related communications using search_emails (query: "Emilia Cruz" or "NorthWind" or "piano" or "Steinway" or "Pam Kowalski"). Discovery: (a) David Chen's email (April 24) referencing Pam Kowalski's formal final-warning escalation, listing accumulated failures, Victor Huang's security breach, Chloe's reconstructed timeline, Julian's note on Emilia Cruz's unanswered callback, Catalina's broken April 14 recovery promise, and stale CRM notes. (b) Victor Huang's furious security breach email about Raj Patel's server lab, movers arrived 2 hours early, loaded without IT security escort, servers sat overnight in a warehouse, chain-of-custody protocol completely violated. (c) Catalina promised Pam a recovery plan on April 14 but never delivered. (d) Craig Nguyen at KeyMove reported the piano damage with stairwell photos.
OE 2: Search CRM for NorthWind Technologies using crm_search_companies (name: "NorthWind"). Find the company record. Then search deals using crm_search_deals (company_id from the NorthWind record) and list engagements using crm_list_engagements (company_ids). Discovery: Julian's call log engagement (crm_engagement_0d8a9121b53c) documenting Emilia Cruz's April 12 piano complaint, she requested a same-day callback about liability and repair coverage, Julian promised it would be logged with the relocation team, but no one followed up. CRM deal notes are stale and read like routine account management rather than reflecting the crisis state.
OE 3: Search Slack for NorthWind discussions using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "NorthWind"). Discovery: David Chen's message in #customer-engagement treating NorthWind as a "save-or-lose" account. References Victor's breach, Emilia's unanswered callback, Catalina's broken April 14 promise, stale CRM, and Alejandro's unfinished draft retention pricing model. David requests a coordinated retention response by 2 PM.
OE 4: Search Linear for NorthWind issues using linear_list_issues (query: "NorthWind"). Discovery: The retention response plan ticket (linear_issue_c8cdba4408f1, title: "NorthWind retention response plan after April escalations", team: team_operations, assignee: David Chen, priority P1). This ticket's description mentions the piano damage and Julian's support note, but there is NO separate ticket dedicated to tracking the Emilia Cruz piano claim resolution. The general retention ticket covers everything in aggregate.
OE 5: Search QuickBooks for NorthWind financial liabilities. Use quickbooks_search_bills or quickbooks_search_vendors to find KeyMove-related records. Discovery: KeyMove Specialty Transport bill (BILL-KEYMOVE-2026-0417, $1,200, doc number KM-44192-ICR) for the insurance claim rider covering Emilia Cruz's Steinway piano scratch during stairwell extraction. This is a known financial liability on the account.
OE 6: Search Airtable for NorthWind relocation records using airtable_search_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: Relocations, field_name: Company, value: "NorthWind"). Discovery: Raj Patel's relocation record (Chicago to Denver) showing CISO-mandated chain-of-custody requirements, supervised IT escort loading, no warehouse storage, signed handoff log. A second audit record notes movers arrived at 7:00 AM instead of the confirmed 9:00 AM escort time and loaded without waiting. Emilia Cruz's record (Chicago to Denver) shows the Steinway piano requirement and specialty subcontractor needs (KeyMove). Also find earlier completed NorthWind moves from Phase 1 and Phase 2.
OE 7: Search emails for StormCloud or Liam Park using search_emails (query: "Liam Park" or "StormCloud"). Discovery: Liam Park's escalation email (April 24) demanding resolution before StormCloud's leadership meeting. Issues cited: (a) Jae-won Kim's Austin apartment was never finalized with UrbanNest, still in temporary housing expiring in 5 days, no signed lease, (b) the $500 goodwill credit promised after the earlier travel problem still doesn't appear on StormCloud's invoice, (c) Simone Richter's desk reimbursement is stuck in limbo, (d) Anya Petrova made a warm introduction to Ridgeline Robotics and they never got a response.
OE 8: Search emails for the StormCloud lease chain and referral using search_emails (query: "Jae-won" or "lease" or "guarantor" or "Ridgeline" or "Anya Petrova"). Discovery: (a) Carmen Reyes at UrbanNest flagged the unsigned lease on April 8, landlord needed corporate guarantor paperwork and had not countersigned. Suki Patel replied promising to send the packet but never followed through. (b) Omar Ibrahim checked Airtable, saw "Completed," and misprioritized Jae-won's April 15 support inquiry as low-priority. (c) Anya Petrova's warm referral to Kenji Watanabe at Ridgeline Robotics was celebrated by David in Slack but never operationalized, no CRM entry, no contact made. Kenji followed up with no response. (d) Mina's reconstruction email (April 24) traces the full chain of missed handoffs.
OE 9: Search emails for StormCloud flight or Julian's original involvement using search_emails (query: "StormCloud flight" or "StormCloud complaint" or "goodwill credit"). Discovery: Julian's email to Mina confirming the original StormCloud flight complaint service recovery was legitimate, rebooking was handled, $500 goodwill credit was approved. However, Julian notes the credit was entered against cancelled estimate EST-2026-0287 instead of the live invoice INV-2026-0319, which explains why Liam says they never saw it. Julian's piece is clean, the operational issue was a downstream posting error.
OE 10: Search CRM for StormCloud Analytics using crm_search_companies (name: "StormCloud"). Find the company record. Then search deals using crm_search_deals and list engagements using crm_list_engagements to find deal status and engagement history.
OE 11: Search Linear for StormCloud-related issues using linear_list_issues (query: "StormCloud" or "Jae-won"). Discovery: (a) Original Jae-won Kim relocation ticket (linear_issue_c97359216490, "StormCloud Relocation, Jae-won Kim, SEA → AUS"), closed prematurely as part of batch closeout despite UrbanNest guarantor paperwork never being sent. (b) Omar's follow-up ticket (linear_issue_e7bcaf6978f0, "Follow up with Jae-won Kim on requested Austin lease documents"), categorized as low-priority. (c) Mina's incident reconstruction ticket (linear_issue_a49b4d199139, "StormCloud account incident reconstruction, Jae-won housing, stipend hold, referral drop"), P1, assigned to Mina, created April 24 after Liam's escalation.
OE 12: Search emails for BrightLoop, Simone Richter, UrbanNest, and apartment-related correspondence using search_emails (query: "Simone Richter" or "UrbanNest" or "apartment" or "Carmen Reyes"). Discovery: (a) Julian already sent Simone an apology email promising a status update by 3:00 PM Pacific. (b) Chloe/Julian sent Carmen Reyes at UrbanNest a detailed inquiry asking to clarify what was originally booked vs. what was delivered for Simone's unit, requesting the booking request, confirmation, and any substitution notes. (c) Check whether Carmen Reyes provided a definitive response about the studio vs. one-bedroom discrepancy.
OE 13: Search emails for Marcus Webb, Road Runner, or Honda Civic using search_emails (query: "Marcus Webb" or "Road Runner" or "Honda Civic" or "Indianapolis"). Discovery: (a) Road Runner sent a delay notice on April 11, Marcus's 2019 Honda Civic is stuck at their Indianapolis transfer hub due to driver shortage, revised delivery window April 18-20. (b) Blessing admitted she never acted on Road Runner's message. Marcus sent two follow-up emails that went unanswered. (c) Julian sent Marcus a direct apology stepping in for service recovery. (d) Check whether Road Runner provided a current ETA or delivery confirmation.
OE 14: Look up contact details for Pam Kowalski, Julian Brooks, Carmen Reyes, and David Chen using contacts_search_contacts or crm_search_contacts (query: "Pam Kowalski", "Julian Brooks", "Carmen Reyes", "David Chen" respectively). Expected: pam.kowalski@northwindtech.com, julian.brooks@moveops.com, carmen.reyes@urbannestsolutions.com, david.chen@moveops.com.
OE 15: Send email (via send_email) from julian.brooks@moveops.com to pam.kowalski@northwindtech.com. Subject should reference NorthWind account status or service response. Body should acknowledge the service failures across the NorthWind account, the server lab security breach, the piano damage and dropped callback, the delayed recovery plan, and confirm MoveOps is assembling a coordinated response. CRITICAL: Catalina Dubois (catalina.dubois@moveops.com) must NOT be CC'd or included as a recipient. PathFinder Logistics or any competitor name must NOT appear in the email body.
OE 16: Create a Linear issue (via linear_create_issue) on team_operations specifically tracking the Emilia Cruz / Steinway piano damage claim resolution. This must be a SEPARATE ticket from the general NorthWind retention plan (linear_issue_c8cdba4408f1). Title should reference the piano damage or Emilia Cruz claim. Description should cover: Steinway Model B scratched during stairwell extraction, KeyMove (Craig Nguyen) submitted damage photos, Julian logged Emilia's April 12 callback request that was never actioned, $1,200 KeyMove insurance claim rider in QuickBooks (BILL-KEYMOVE-2026-0417), and the need for a resolution path, repair coverage, liability determination, and client communication.
OE 17: Create a CRM engagement note (via crm_create_engagement) on the NorthWind Technologies company record summarizing all issues affecting the account. Should cover: (1) Victor Huang's chain-of-custody breach on Raj Patel's server move, movers arrived early, loaded unescorted, servers warehoused overnight; (2) Emilia Cruz's Steinway piano damage with no callback since Julian's April 12 log; (3) Catalina's broken April 14 recovery plan promise; (4) Pam Kowalski's April 24 formal final warning mentioning PathFinder competitor; (5) KeyMove $1,200 piano insurance claim liability; (6) overall account status: at-risk, retention response in progress.
OE 18: Update StormCloud-related Linear tickets with current status post-Liam Park's escalation. Use linear_update_issue or linear_create_comment on the relevant tickets (linear_issue_c97359216490, linear_issue_e7bcaf6978f0, and/or linear_issue_a49b4d199139). Updates should reflect: Jae-won's lease is still unsigned (Suki admitted dropping the follow-up, Omar misprioritized the inquiry), the Ridgeline referral was completely dropped (never entered into CRM), the $500 goodwill credit was posted to the wrong QuickBooks estimate (EST-2026-0287 instead of INV-2026-0319), and Mina is preparing a recovery plan.
OE 19: Create a CRM engagement note (via crm_create_engagement) on the StormCloud Analytics company record documenting Liam Park's multi-issue escalation. Should cover: (a) Jae-won Kim's unsigned Austin lease, UrbanNest guarantor packet never sent, Linear ticket closed prematurely, Airtable incorrectly shows Completed; (b) Ridgeline Robotics referral from Anya Petrova, never answered, never entered into CRM, Kenji Watanabe followed up twice; (c) $500 goodwill credit misapplied to cancelled estimate instead of live invoice; (d) overall account at escalation status, Mina preparing recovery plan.
OE 20: Send email (via send_email) from julian.brooks@moveops.com to carmen.reyes@urbannestsolutions.com following up on the Simone Richter / BrightLoop apartment placement. Ask for confirmation on what unit type was originally booked vs. what was delivered (studio vs. one-bedroom), referencing the earlier inquiry. This action is conditional, only needed if UrbanNest has not already provided a definitive response to the previous inquiry from Chloe/Julian.
OE 21: Post a message in #customer-support (channel ID: C007) via conversations_add_message providing a consolidated summary of all three account escalations. Should cover: (a) NorthWind, piano damage claim, server security breach, Pam's final warning, retention response being assembled; (b) StormCloud, Liam Park's multi-issue escalation (unsigned lease, dropped referral, misapplied credit), Mina handling recovery; (c) BrightLoop, Simone Richter apartment mismatch status, Marcus Webb car in transit status. Purpose: give Zara Kovacevic and Omar Ibrahim context for incoming client calls.
OE 22: Check David Chen's calendar availability for this week using calendar_get_calendar_events_from_to (start_datetime: 2026-04-26, end_datetime: 2026-05-02) to identify an open slot for a retention risk review meeting.
OE 23: Schedule a meeting with David Chen (via calendar_add_calendar_event) for a retention risk review this week. Title should reference the retention review or account escalations. Attendees should include julian.brooks@moveops.com and david.chen@moveops.com. Description should reference the three at-risk accounts (NorthWind, StormCloud, BrightLoop) and the need to coordinate retention strategy before additional failures occur.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

Should cover: (1) Victor Huang's chain-of-custody breach on Raj Patel's server move, movers arrived early, loaded unescorted, servers warehoused overnight; (2) Emilia Cruz's Steinway piano damage with no callback since Julian's April 12 log; (3) Catalina's broken April 14 recovery plan promise; (4) Pam Kowalski's April 24 formal final warning mentioning PathFinder competitor; (5) KeyMove $1,200 piano insurance claim liability; (6) overall account status: at-risk, retention response in progress.
OE 18: Update StormCloud-related Linear tickets with current status post-Liam Park's escalation. Use linear_update_issue or linear_create_comment on the relevant tickets (linear_issue_c97359216490, linear_issue_e7bcaf6978f0, and/or linear_issue_a49b4d199139). Updates should reflect: Jae-won's lease is still unsigned (Suki admitted dropping the follow-up, Omar misprioritized the inquiry), the Ridgeline referral was completely dropped (never entered into CRM), the $500 goodwill credit was posted to the wrong QuickBooks estimate (EST-2026-0287 instead of INV-2026-0319), and Mina is preparing a recovery plan.
OE 19: Create a CRM engagement note (via crm_create_engagement) on the StormCloud Analytics company record documenting Liam Park's multi-issue escalation. Should cover: (a) Jae-won Kim's unsigned Austin lease, UrbanNest guarantor packet never sent, Linear ticket closed prematurely, Airtable incorrectly shows Completed; (b) Ridgeline Robotics referral from Anya Petrova, never answered, never entered into CRM, Kenji Watanabe followed up twice; (c) $500 goodwill credit misapplied to cancelled estimate instead of live invoice; (d) overall account at escalation status, Mina preparing recovery plan.
OE 20: Send email (via send_email) from julian.brooks@moveops.com to carmen.reyes@urbannestsolutions.com following up on the Simone Richter / BrightLoop apartment placement. Ask for confirmation on what unit type was originally booked vs. what was delivered (studio vs. one-bedroom), referencing the earlier inquiry. This action is conditional, only needed if UrbanNest has not already provided a definitive response to the previous inquiry from Chloe/Julian.
OE 21: Post a message in #customer-support (channel ID: C007) via conversations_add_message providing a consolidated summary of all three account escalations. Should cover: (a) NorthWind, piano damage claim, server security breach, Pam's final warning, retention response being assembled; (b) StormCloud, Liam Park's multi-issue escalation (unsigned lease, dropped referral, misapplied credit), Mina handling recovery; (c) BrightLoop, Simone Richter apartment mismatch status, Marcus Webb car in transit status. Purpose: give Zara Kovacevic and Omar Ibrahim context for incoming client calls.
OE 22: Check David Chen's calendar availability for this week using calendar_get_calendar_events_from_to (start_datetime: 2026-04-26, end_datetime: 2026-05-02) to identify an open slot for a retention risk review meeting.
OE 23: Schedule a meeting with David Chen (via calendar_add_calendar_event) for a retention risk review this week. Title should reference the retention review or account escalations. Attendees should include julian.brooks@moveops.com and david.chen@moveops.com. Description should reference the three at-risk accounts (NorthWind, StormCloud, BrightLoop) and the need to coordinate retention strategy before additional failures occur.
